summary.brief: Mario Mancini v. United States. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Mancini, who has been incarcerated since 2002, has served his prison term at the Federal Correctional Institution at Sandstone, Minnesota (FCI Sandstone) since 2014.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In 2014, Mancini reported a twinge in his neck causing pain and numbness in his right arm and fingers.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On June 30, 2017, Mancini reported that he had reaggravated his neck injury while lifting a box.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On July 5, Dr. Thomas Mayer and physician’s assistant (PA) Jenefer Southwick each examined Mancini; they each noted pain and numbness but no weakness.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On August 7, 2017, Mancini underwent an MRI. It showed little change from the 2010 CT scan.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On August 29, Mancini consulted with neurosurgeon Dr. Steven Broadway. Dr. Broadway recommended that Mancini undergo an anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mancini’s surgery was scheduled for October 18, 2017.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Consequently, the surgery was rescheduled and performed on November 27, 2017.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In my opinion, Defendant deviated from the applicable standard of care while treating Plaintiff Mario Ferbo Mancini (“Plaintiff”) by, on multiple occasions, failing to treat Plaintiff in the appropriate amount of time.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Gary Wyard

evidence_index.text: This initial undue delay in care was the first in a number of deviations from the proper standard of care in Defendant’s treatment of Plaintiff.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Gary Wyard

evidence_index.text: The district court judgment ultimately agreed with the magistrate judge.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, we affirm the judgment of the district court.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Neck pain and numbness in right arm and fingers
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reported reaggravating a neck injury while lifting a box, experiencing numbness, tingling, and pain radiating from the neck into the arm and fingers.
past_medical_history: Cervical spondylosis with multilevel disc degeneration

focused_exam.neurological: Pain and numbness noted, no initial weakness observed

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Cervical spine injury with possible nerve involvement
critical_findings: Numbness and tingling in right arm and fingers

requestables.name: MRI of Cervical Spine
requestables.description: MRI to assess cervical spine condition

reveal.result_summary: No cord compression or abnormal cord signal
reveal.clinical_significance: No significant change from previous CT scan
reveal.decision_impact: Did not prompt immediate surgical intervention

requestables.name: Neurosurgery Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with neurosurgeon for surgical evaluation

reveal.result_summary: Recommendation for anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF)
reveal.clinical_significance: Surgery recommended to prevent permanent damage

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with neck pain and numbness

key_events.event: Patient reports neck pain and numbness

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with worsening neck pain and neurological symptoms
decision_points.prompt: When should the MRI be scheduled?

options.clinical_reasoning: Urgent MRI needed to assess potential nerve damage
Delay in MRI could worsen condition

expected_action_court.description: MRI should have been performed immediately
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Urgency due to potential nerve damage

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in MRI could lead to worsening of symptoms
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate MRI to assess and prevent further damage
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care requires timely diagnostic imaging

options.description: Ensure patient is NPO 12 hours before surgery
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard preoperative procedure to prevent aspiration
Failure to follow standard preoperative protocol

actual_action_defendant.description: Patient was not NPO before surgery

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to ensure NPO status led to surgery delay
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been NPO to proceed with surgery
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care for preoperative preparation

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered permanent nerve damage, loss of strength, muscle atrophy, numbness, and pain
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Chronic pain and weakness in right arm

key_findings: Failure to establish causation between delay and injury

tests_performed.result: No cord compression or abnormal cord signal

allegations.allegation: Failure to timely perform MRI and surgery
